name: Kaleidoscope Cookies
recipeIngredient: Several drops green and red Food coloring
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter or margarine in a large mixing bowl with an electric mixer on medium to high speed for 30 seconds.
2. Add sugar and baking powder, and beat until combined.
3. Beat in egg and vanilla until combined.
4. Beat in as much flour as possible with the mixer.
5. Stir any remaining flour with a wooden spoon.
6. Divide dough into three equal portions.
7. To one portion, add peppermint extract and green food coloring; mix until combined and set aside.
8. Add red food coloring to another portion; mix until combined and set aside.
9. Leave the remaining dough plain. Cover dough and chill at least 1 hour or until easy to handle.
10. Shape red dough into four 8-inch long rolls.
11. Divide the plain dough in half.
12. On a lightly floured surface, roll each half of plain dough into an 8x6-inch rectangle.
13. Place one red and one green lengthwise on each rectangle.
14. Stack a roll of the alternate color atop each roll.
15. Bring up the plain dough around the stack and seal it.
16. Wrap rolls of dough in freezer paper.
17. Store in freezer for up to 6 months.
18. For easier slicing, let the rolls thaw in the refrigerator several hours or overnight.
19. To bake: Slice rolls into 1/4-inch thick slices.
20. Place on an ungreased cookie sheet.
21. Bake in a 375°F oven about 8 minutes, or until dough just begins to brown.
22. Remove cookies; cool on wire racks.
23. Makes about 60 cookies. Source: BH&G Christmas Cookies 1995.
